negative_cases.why_wrong: Param Given Gain Select: keeps the file compilable but forces the observable output path low, so it can pass include/instance smoke checks while failing the required behavior to The default instance must use unity gain and report metric 0 because gain was not explicitly provided.

Param Given Gain Select: keeps the main operator syntax but collapses the computed value through the wrong condition, so coarse smoke cases may run while boundary and waveform checks for Use $param_given() to choose behavior based on parameter override presence fail.
Param Given Gain Select: introduces incorrect initialization or state bias, so startup and reset-sensitive checks fail while the model still compiles and produces activity.

Param Given Gain Select: offsets the secondary metric or observable path, so the primary waveform may look plausible while calibrated checker evidence for The default instance must use unity gain and report metric 0 because gain was not explicitly provided fails.

Param Given Gain Select: scales the final observable response, so compile and rough activity checks can pass while calibrated output checks for Use $param_given() to choose behavior based on parameter override presence fail.
